title: Emergence of Transfer Learning towards Specific Identification of Alzheimer’s Disease – A Prospective Approach
description: This conference paper investigates the effectiveness of transfer learning techniques for the early and specific identification of Alzheimer’s Disease using medical imaging data. Pre-trained deep learning models are fine-tuned on domain-specific datasets to improve diagnostic accuracy while reducing training complexity and data dependency. The study highlights how transfer learning can enhance feature extraction in neuroimaging, enabling reliable detection of disease patterns at early stages and supporting clinical decision-making.

title: Prospects and Challenges in UAV-Based Communication for Disaster Management
description: The paper proposes a hybrid UAV communication architecture (Satellite + LoRa + FANET) to ensure reliable, low-latency, and energy-efficient connectivity during disaster response operations.

title: Design and Development of a Multi-Functional Interactive Robot with Handshake, AI Voice Assistance, Projection, and Mobility
description: This journal article presents the design and implementation of a multi-functional interactive robot capable of physical interaction through handshaking, AI-based voice assistance, visual projection, and autonomous mobility. The system integrates embedded control, speech recognition, and motion planning to enable natural human–robot interaction. The proposed architecture demonstrates the feasibility of deploying low-cost, intelligent service robots for educational, assistive, and public interaction environments.

title: An ESP32-Based Morse Code Transmission System Using Telegram Bot
description: This paper introduces a Morse code transmission system utilizing an ESP32 microcontroller and displaying the decoded message via a Telegram bot in real-time. In contrast to the traditional Morse code inputting methods, which are normally based on a single button with timing distinction between dot and dash, the suggested design utilizes two special buttons for dot and dash, respectively, and another button for sending the entire message.

title: An Intelligent Framework for Real-Time Yoga Pose Detection and Posture Correction
description: This paper presents a hybrid Edge AI based framework for real time yoga pose detection and posture correction. The proposed system integrates lightweight human pose estimation models with biomechanical feature extraction and a CNN LSTM based temporal learning architecture to recognize yoga poses and analyze motion dynamics. Joint angles and skeletal features are computed from detected keypoints and compared with reference pose configurations to evaluate posture correctness.

title: RoboDoc: An Autonomous Medical Robot for Primary Health Assessment in Villages
description: This paper introduces RoboDoc, an autonomous medical robot designed to provide primary health assessments in rural and underserved village areas. The system integrates biomedical sensors, embedded processing, and decision-support algorithms to measure vital health parameters such as heart rate, temperature, and blood pressure. RoboDoc aims to bridge healthcare accessibility gaps by enabling preliminary diagnosis, data logging, and remote consultation support in regions with limited medical infrastructure.

title: A Deep Neural Network Model for The Detection of Breast Cancer
description: This conference paper proposes a deep neural network–based model for the automated detection of breast cancer using clinical and imaging datasets. The model leverages multilayer feature learning to distinguish between benign and malignant cases with high accuracy. The study emphasizes the role of deep learning in improving early diagnosis, reducing human error, and assisting healthcare professionals in making informed diagnostic decisions.

title: Use of Artificial Intelligence in Engineering
description: This book provides a comprehensive overview of the application of artificial intelligence across various engineering domains. It covers fundamental AI concepts, machine learning techniques, and real-world case studies in areas such as automation, robotics, healthcare, and smart systems. The book serves as a practical reference for students, researchers, and professionals seeking to understand how AI-driven solutions can enhance efficiency, innovation, and decision-making in modern engineering.

title: IoT-based Heat Stress Adaptive Crop Recommendation System
description: This patent describes an Internet of Things (IoT)–based system designed to monitor environmental and crop parameters to address heat stress in agriculture. The system collects real-time data such as temperature, humidity, and soil conditions using distributed sensors and applies intelligent algorithms to recommend adaptive crop management strategies. The proposed solution aims to improve crop yield, resilience, and sustainability under changing climatic conditions.

title: Smart T-Shirt with Medical Monitoring
description: This patent presents a smart wearable T-shirt embedded with biomedical sensors for continuous health monitoring. The system is capable of tracking vital parameters such as heart rate, body temperature, and oxygen saturation, with integrated data processing and alert mechanisms. The smart T-shirt is designed for healthcare, elderly monitoring, and emergency response applications, offering a comfortable and non-invasive solution for real-time medical supervision.

title: Medicinal Plant Identification and Classification Of Himalayan Region - A UAV Integrated Deep Learning Approach
description: This technology provides a deep learning system that incorporates Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs) to automatically recognize and classify medicinal plants in the Himalaya. The UAV can take high-quality geotagged images of plants in remote areas with its GPS, onboard edge processing, and high-definition camera. The drone is responsible for initial processing and the final processing for classification is performed on the cloud using a ResNet-152 V2 model.

title: An intelligent fire fighting system to save forests with early notifications
description: The current invention reveals a smart forest fire system intervention, anticipation and early warning system based on Internet of Things (IoT), and machine learning with the aim of dealing with the increasing risk of uncontrolled forest fires. The forest fires are very unpredictable and spread very fast because there is variation in some environmental parameters in terms of temperature, humidity, soil moisture, vegetation dryness, and human activities.

title: IoT-based smart solar charging bag with integrated security and emergency features
description: The IoT-enabled smart solar charging bag integrates exterior high-efficiency solar panels, a buck-boost power conditioning module, a removable battery bank, multiple charging interfaces including USB-C, USB-A, Micro-USB and Qi wireless charging, and an ESP32-based IoT controller with integrated GSM and GPS modules; the controller monitors solar generation, voltage and current, and battery state, displays data on an on-board OLED and transmits telemetry via GSM to a mobile application

title: TinyML-Enabled IoT Systems: Recent Advancements in Tiny Machine Learnin
description: The book TinyML-Enabled IoT Systems: Recent Advancement in Tiny Machine Learning presents a comprehensive exploration of Tiny Machine Learning (TinyML), an emerging field that enables machine learning models to run efficiently on ultra-low- power embedded and edge devices.

title: Introduction to TinyML: The New Era of Low-Power AI for IoT Devices
description: This chapter presents a comprehensive introduction to TinyML, examining its evolution from conventional cloud-centric AI and Edge AI architectures toward distributed embedded intelligence. The chapter discusses the fundamental architecture of TinyML systems, key model optimization and deployment techniques, including quantization, pruning, and model compression, as well as hardware-aware design strategies for efficient on-device inference.

title: Algorithmic Foundations of TinyML for Resource-Constrained IoT Devices
description: This chapter presents an exhaustive analysis of the algorithms that can be used in the context of TinyML, with emphasis on the essential characteristics of the algorithms, which include their resourcefulness, low power consumption, computational simplicity, real-time processing, robustness against noisy data, and interpretability of results.
